rationale: The cited paper is discussed repeatedly with a specific role rather than as a generic literature-list reference. The citing paper uses Jap and Haruvy (2008) to motivate that nonprice relational value can shape bidding aggression, then explicitly positions its own contribution as moving beyond that relationship-based account to bidder-quality inference from bid-level dynamics. It also contrasts the empirical basis directly (self-reported relational measures in prior work versus point-by-point bid data here). This is a clear build-on use.
relation_description: The 2013 paper builds on Jap and Haruvy (2008)'s evidence that nonprice relationship factors affect bidding aggressiveness in industrial reverse auctions. It extends that line by introducing a dynamic bid-adjustment model that infers relative bidder quality from observed competitive bids rather than relying on relationship self-reports.
